role_title: Senior Associate, Marketplace Audience Strategy & Operations

location.office_time_required: some time in-office and some time remote but it is quite flexible

level_signals: 3+ years of work experience in strategy, operations, planning, or other related areas
Help manage a team of College Ambassadors and leads
Base pay range $87,000–$128,000 USD consistent with mid-level associate

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 3+ years of work experience in strategy, operations, planning, or other related areas

You're analytical – You enjoy getting deep into data and feel comfortable translating what you're seeing into tangible insights. You use a variety of AI tools (e.g. Cursor) to improve analytics tooling
You're action-oriented – You have a bias towards action, are comfortable with ambiguity, and are able to thrive in a fast-paced, constantly changing work environment
You're creative – You love thinking about new problems, and don't resort to a "this is how it's always been done" thinking.

You're a collaborator and relationship builder – You are an excellent communicator and eager to build relationships with teammates and cross-functional partners. You have an ability to lead with empathy while also holding cross functional partners accountable for key deliverables

scope.surface: US Marketplace Growth — College students and Gen Z audience segment; College Ambassador program management
scope.business_model: marketplace / consumer platform (food and goods delivery)
primary_metrics_inferred: Business performance trends in US Marketplace
Reporting efficiency gains from AI tooling
core_responsibilities: Dive into data to explain performance at the lowest level of detail
Use AI tools to drive reporting efficiency
Work with cross-functional partners to report out and explain performance
Help manage a team of College Ambassadors and leads who support on-the-ground presence at colleges
Think through how to solve blockers to performance at individual College campuses
Build reporting and performance updates for senior leadership
Think up new ideas and translate them into an actionable test or new reporting mechanism
Drive the creation of strategic plans that drive execution throughout the business

green_flags: Clear audience ownership (College / Gen Z) within a large business unit
Position fill-by date stated (6/27/2026), indicating active hiring
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail
red_flags: No specific cities listed for hybrid location — unclear which offices are involved
Visa sponsorship not addressed anywhere in the JD
Role involves people management (College Ambassadors) but is titled 'Senior Associate' — scope/title mismatch may affect leveling expectations
Nice-to-haves section is entirely absent, making it harder to distinguish hard from soft expectations
